pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 12 defines PROJECT_DIR=/home/ga/AndroidStudioProjects/SunflowerApp; export_result.sh line 6 uses same path
setup_task.sh line 34 BUILD_FILE=$PROJECT_DIR/app/build.gradle.kts; file verified in local data directory

setup_task.sh line 51 sed command enforces versionName='1.0.0' in build.gradle.kts. Original data (SunflowerApp/app/build.gradle.kts) contains versionName='1.0' but setup modifies it to '1.0.0'. Metadata describes the expected state AFTER setup initialization.
Local SunflowerApp data (SunflowerApp/app/build.gradle.kts line 14): versionCode = 1; setup_task.sh line 50 enforces versionCode=1 via sed
Local SunflowerApp data (SunflowerApp/app/build.gradle.kts line 12): minSdk = 24; setup_task.sh line 52 enforces minSdk=24 via sed
Local SunflowerApp data (SunflowerApp/app/build.gradle.kts line 13): targetSdk = 34; setup_task.sh line 53 enforces targetSdk=34 via sed
Web search confirms official Android Sunflower repository at github.com/android/sunflower - a real, Google-maintained open-source Android app demonstrating best practices with Jetpack Compose

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the official Android Sunflower application (https://github.com/android/sunflower) as its real-world data source. The local copy resides at examples/android_studio_env/data/SunflowerApp. During task initialization, setup_task.sh copies this to /home/ga/AndroidStudioProjects/SunflowerApp and enforces specific SDK values via sed: minSdk=24, targetSdk=34, versionCode=1. The versionName is modified from the original '1.0' to '1.0.0'. The task expects to generate a JSON file at app/src/main/assets/build-info.json containing build metadata extracted from the modified build.gradle.kts configuration.
